goal: Give Alex one self-contained Python snippet (paste into Script Editor, hit Play) that slides a cube back and forth along X forever.
thoughts: Single code block, <=15 lines, no OmniGraph, no Behavior Scripts, no extension install. Use omni.kit.app update-event stream or a timeline subscription. Set xformOp:translate on each tick using pxr.UsdGeom + Gf. Comments: one per logical block, not every line.
code:
# 1. Create the cube (or Alex can drag one from Create > Shapes > Cube)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Slider",
    prim_type="Cube",
    translation=(0.0, 0.0, 0.5)
)

# 2. Wire up a per-frame updater. Paste into Script Editor, hit Play.
run_usd_script(script="""
import omni.kit.app
from pxr import Usd, UsdGeom, Gf
import math

stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()
cube = UsdGeom.Xformable(stage.GetPrimAtPath("/World/Slider"))

t0 = [0.0]
def on_update(e):
    t0[0] += 1.0/60.0
    x = 0.5 + 0.5 * math.sin(t0[0] * 2.0)   # 0..1 back and forth
    cube.ClearXformOpOrder()
    cube.AddTranslateOp().Set(Gf.Vec3d(x, 0.0, 0.5))

_sub = omni.kit.app.get_app().get_update_event_stream().create_subscription_to_pop(on_update)
""")

# Hit Play. If nothing moves, check the timeline is actually advancing.
failure_modes: Requiring an extension install — Alex bails
Introducing OmniGraph / Action Graph / Behavior Scripts
Forgetting ClearXformOpOrder() — stacking translate ops each tick accumulates drift
Missing 'hit Play' reminder — Alex thinks the code is broken when timeline is paused
